examples.title: Blockchain Structure
examples.description: Demonstrates the basic structure of a blockchain
examples.code:
CLASS Block
 HEADER: Hash
 BODY: List<Transaction>
 PREV_HASH: Hash
 TIMESTAMP: Timestamp
 NONCE: Integer
END CLASS

CLASS Blockchain
 CHAIN: List<Block>
 ADD_BLOCK(Block newBlock)
 IF newBlock.PREV_HASH == CHAIN[-1].HEADER
 CHAIN.APPEND(newBlock)
 END IF
 END ADD_BLOCK
END CLASS
examples.explanation: This pseudocode defines a Block class and a Blockchain class. The Block class has attributes for the block's header, body, previous hash, timestamp, and nonce. The Blockchain class has a list of blocks and a method to add a new block to the chain.

examples.title: Blockchain Implementation
examples.description: A basic implementation of a blockchain in Python
examples.explanation: This Python code defines a Block class and a Blockchain class. The Block class has attributes for the block's index, previous hash, timestamp, data, and hash. The Blockchain class has a list of blocks and methods to create a genesis block, get the latest block, and add a new block to the chain.

examples.title: Blockchain Implementation
examples.description: A basic implementation of a blockchain in C
examples.explanation: This C code defines a Block struct and a Blockchain struct. The Block struct has attributes for the block's index, previous hash, timestamp, data, and hash. The Blockchain struct has a pointer to a list of blocks and an integer for the length of the chain. The code also includes functions to calculate the hash of a block and to add a new block to the chain.
